question:
Find the minimum value of the linear program: minimize z = d^T * y 
where d = [-12.   2.], subject to 
M * y <= h, with 
M = [[ 3. -1.]
 [ 3.  1.]
 [ 3. -3.]
 [ 0. -2.]
 [ 2. -1.]] and 
h = [-1.  7. -6. -5. -2.]. What is the optimal objective value?

Consider the optimization problem: minimize f(w) = p^T * w 
with p = [ -5. -18.  -6.  19.], under the constraints 
G * w <= q, where 
G = [[ 2.  4.  1. -4.]
 [-3. -1.  0.  4.]
 [-3.  2.  2. -3.]
 [ 1.  1.  3.  4.]
 [-1. -2.  1.  2.]
 [-4. -3. -1.  4.]] and 
q = [ 18.  -3.  10.  25.  -1. -12.]. What is the minimum value achieved?

Consider the optimization problem: minimize f(w) = p^T * w

 [ 0. -1.  1.]
 [ 4. -3. -1.]
 [-4. -4.  3.]
 [-3. -3.  4.]
 [-4. -3.  4.]
 [-2.  4. -3.]
 [ 3.  0.  3.]] and 
q = [  6.  -1.  -7. -10.  -4.  -5.   4.  11.]. What is the minimum value achieved?
